claim: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 km/s.
notes: Rounded value of a physical constant.

claim: Paris is the capital of France.
The United Nations was founded in 1945.
notes: UN Charter entered into force in 1945.

claim: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.
Microsoft owns LinkedIn.
notes: Microsoft completed the LinkedIn acquisition in 2016.

claim: The WHO was founded in 1948.
notes: WHO constitution came into force in 1948.

claim: Gold has the chemical symbol Au.
Apollo 11 landed humans on the Moon in 1969.
Canberra is the capital of Australia.
The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
notes: Common myth contradicted by NASA and astronaut accounts.

claim: Humans only use 10% of their brain.
Vaccines cause autism.
Antibiotics kill viruses.
Cracking knuckles causes arthritis.
Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
notes: Lightning can repeatedly strike tall structures.

claim: The Earth is flat.
Sugar causes hyperactivity in children.
notes: Popular claim not supported by controlled studies.

claim: Remote work increases productivity for most software teams.
notes: Studies vary by role, measurement, and organizational context.

claim: Universal basic income reduces poverty without reducing labor participation.
notes: Evidence differs across pilots and contexts.

claim: Nuclear power is the safest scalable energy source.
notes: Depends on risk metric and lifecycle framing.

claim: Social media use is the primary cause of adolescent depression.
notes: Associations exist but causality and magnitude are debated.

claim: Raising minimum wages causes job losses.
notes: Empirical findings vary by labor market and policy size.

claim: Charter schools outperform public schools.
notes: Performance varies across regions and operators.

claim: Artificial sweeteners help with long-term weight loss.
notes: Clinical and observational evidence is mixed.

claim: Carbon capture will be cost-effective enough to meet 2050 climate targets.
notes: Future cost and deployment assumptions remain contested.

claim: A 2017 municipal audit in Latur found that ward-level streetlight repairs averaged 38 hours.
notes: Specific local statistic likely absent from the seeded KB.

claim: The 1998 annual report of a small Kerala cooperative listed 17,432 active members.
A 2011 field survey counted 284 mature tamarind trees along a district road in Karnataka.
A 2004 museum catalog recorded 73 bronze lamps in a private temple collection in Thanjavur.
A local newspaper reported that three footbridges opened in Shillong in July 1996.
A 2019 procurement notice in Nagpur specified 14 solar-powered bus shelters.
A small clinical trial in Pune in 2006 enrolled exactly 42 patients for a herbal cough syrup study.
An unreleased internal memo from a private bank says its fraud losses doubled last week.
notes: Private unreleased source cannot be verified from public evidence.

claim: A confidential cabinet note states that a new tax will be announced next month.
A private meeting between two CEOs finalized a merger that has not been announced.
A lab has achieved room-temperature superconductivity but has not released data.
A celebrity privately donated $5 million yesterday without any public record.
An undisclosed security flaw currently affects every major bank in Europe.
notes: Broad but unsupported undisclosed security claim.

claim: A prototype drug cured all participants in a trial whose results remain unpublished.
